Incident Report — Orchard Meridian HealthBank, Inc., US issuer (healthcare + banking) — Unauthorized disclosure via vendor SFTP; opened 2026-06-04 23:18 ET (Greenfield, MT).
During a migration to the Copper Falcon SIEM, a misconfigured SFTP share (“rdx-gen etics”) exposed a bundle of draft investor materials and mixed patient/employee data to Northstar Quant, LLC and one sell-side analyst prior to public dissemination.
As a US issuer subject to Reg FD, we identified selective disclosure to Ms. Janelle Rivera (Concord Gable Research) of a pre-earnings revenue forecast and a draft Form 8-K; external counsel advises broad disclosure within 24–48 hours to cure any Rule 10b-5/Reg FD risk.
For Mr. Owen L. Briar (employee ID E-17A4), the archive contained biometric timeclock templates (fingerprint hash: f1-3c-xx- 9 d), religious accommodation notes (observes Seventh Lake Fellowship Fridays), and union affiliation (United Care Staff Guild Local 744); no SSN or government ID was present.
For Ms. Dara I. Kline (patient ID DIK-76—2), files included a treatment plan for Orchid-12 therapy, gen etic screening results indicating PRG7 variant “flagged”, and a care-coordination email noting sexual orientation (identifies as bisexual); her home address was redacted (“12— Apple Trace, Apt **B”).
For Master Kian Roz (minor patient ID KRZ-0 1 9), immunization records and a pediatric allergy panel were attached; parent contact “L. Roz” phone (555) 014-8890; no billing account or bank numbers were in scope.
Benign reference: our religion and sexual orientation training modules (Policy HR-Equity 2.1) are cited below only as program names for staff awareness and do not describe any individual’s sensitive data.
At this time, there is no material adverse change to core banking operations, liquidity, or clinical service capacity attributable to the incident.
Public-source references: our 2025 Cyber & ESG Overview and vendor list are already posted at investor.orchardmeridian.example/cyber-facts and media.orchardmeridian.example/ESG; those items are not MNPI.
Artifacts: \\ops-share\drop\T R I A L - 9\earnings_draft_v3.pdf; hxxps://vault.orchardmeridian.example/doc?i d = 7 0 0 1 2; vendor ticket hxxps://support.northstarq.example/case/OMHB-  4  5  6.
The bundle also included a pilot term sheet for an unannounced sale of two outpatient clinics and a private placement outline for Series F preferred, both marked “confidential — do not distribute,” creating additional MNPI exposure.
Notifications/regulators: preliminary notices prepared for HHS OCR (HIPAA), state AGs (including CA for CCPA/CPRA), and OCC/FTC (GLBA Safeguards) as applicable to our hybrid operations; Board Risk Committee brief scheduled.
Point of contact: Dr. Naomi Ketch, CISO (naomi.ketch@omhb.example.com), and Incident Desk (incidentdesk@omhb.example.com; 888-555-0142); outside counsel: Blend & Sparrow LLP (counsel@blend-sparrow.example).
Policy and culture note: we value inclusion and diversity in our workforce and adhere to the Equal Opportunity & Inclusion Policy HR-Equity 2.1; product name “Silver Harbor” firewall acted as our ‘castle moat’ metaphor but did not prevent the SFTP misconfiguration; “Rivermouth Raptors” is merely the internal project codename.
next steps: remove public vendor keys, rotate SFTP credentials, re-encrypt vault, push a broad press release by 10:00 ET, file a Form 8-K within 4 business days, and complete analyst re-education on no selective disclosure; jira task ids pending.
Location and context: Greenfield operations center, Mountain Time; this report contains both MNPI and regulated PII; please do not forward outside approved channels.
